mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of April 2018 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 in walk-forward test, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp, contradicting the negative direction prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates applied to future equity cash flows. Higher cost of capital reduces present value of corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the tightening cycle matures.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with housing starts (HOUST) expansion signals robust consumer demand and capital investment. This dual demand signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise across consumer-sensitive and construction-linked sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 497559M and HOUST at 1319K units as of March 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST grow simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) rising above 107 signals robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, indicating strong supply-side momentum. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as cyclical equity valuations expand in response to near-term production strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when INDPRO > 107.0.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerating below 150K monthly additions signals weakening labor demand and potential economic slowdown. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations and recession risk premia increase.
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of triggered instances when PAYEMS growth decelerates, or Rank IC > 0.04 contradicting the negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 250 combined with federal funds rate below 2.0% signals real rates remain accommodative despite price pressures. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors benefit from negative real rates and nominal earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.013 and FEDFUNDS at 1.69% as of April 2018
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals strengthening economic fundamentals and corporate earnings expansion. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations expand in response to improved macroeconomic momentum and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17385.831B (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2017
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 shows quarter-over-quarter acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 107 combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.0%) signals supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets price in stagflation risk and earnings compression.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.1665 and UNRATE at 3.9% as of March-April 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances when both INDPRO < 107.0 and UNRATE > 4.0%.
